title: Mitigation of overheating events at communication devices
abstract: A method includes: communicating, on a wireless link (111) between a network node (101) and a communication device (102), first data (2001) at a first data rate (210, 210-1, 210-2); and communicating, on the wireless link (111), at least one uplink control signal (2002) associated with an overheating event (221) at the communication device (102); and in response to said communicating of the at least one uplink control signal (2002): communicating, on the wireless link (111), second data (2001) at a second data rate (210, 210-1, 210-2), wherein the second data rate (210, 210-1, 210-2) is different from the first data rate (210, 210-1, 210-2).
claims:
Claims 1 . A method, comprising: - communicating, on a wireless link (1 1 1 ) between a network node (101 ) and a communication device (102), first data (2001 ) at a first data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2), - communicating, on the wireless link (1 1 1 ), at least one uplink control signal (2002) associated with an overheating event (221 ) at the communication device (102), and - in response to said communicating of the at least one uplink control signal (2002): communicating, on the wireless link (1 1 1 ), second data (2001 ) at a second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2), wherein the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) is different from the first data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2). 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the at least one uplink control signal (2002) includes an indicator indicative of a threshold data rate (212), wherein the second data (2001 ) is communicated at the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) which is implemented in accordance with the threshold data rate (212). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein a difference between the first data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) and the threshold data rate (212) is predefined or indicated by an indicator included in the at least one uplink control signal (2002). 4. The method of claims 2 or 3, wherein the threshold data rate (212) is smaller than a maximum data rate supported by parameters of a communication protocol stack of the wireless link (1 1 1 ) in accordance to a modulation and/or coding scheme used for said communicating of the second data (2001 ). 5. The method of claims 2 or 3, wherein the threshold data rate (212) is a maximum data rate supported by parameters of a communication protocol stack of the wireless link (1 1 1 ) in accordance with by a given level of carrier aggregation. 6. The method of any one of claims 2 - 5, wherein the indicator is indicative of whether the threshold data rate (212) is to be applied to uplink communication and/or downlink communication. 7. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: - implementing the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) by changing a scheduling rate (301 ) of resources allocated for said communicating of the second data (2001 ) if compared to the scheduling rate of resources allocated for said communicating of the first data (2001 ). 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising: - implementing the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) by changing an outflow rate of a transmit data buffer (321 , 322) for said communicating of the second data (2001 ) if compared to the outflow rate of the transmit data buffer (321 , 322) for said communicating of the first data (2001 ). 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one uplink control signal (2002) comprises a sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002), wherein the method further comprises: - incrementally implementing the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) in response to communicating uplink control signals (2002) of the sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002). 10. The method of claim 9, 

 wherein the at least one uplink control signal (2002) includes an indicator indicative of the sign and/or magnitude of the difference between incremental implementations of the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2). 1 1 . The method of claim 10, further comprising: - monitoring a temperature associated with the overheating event (221 ), and - repeatedly adjusting the indicators included in the uplink control signals (2002) of the sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002) based on said monitoring. 12. The method of any one of claims 9 - 1 1 , wherein an average time interval (2098) between adjacent uplink control signals (2002) of the sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002) is not larger than 10 sec, optionally not larger than 1 sec, further optionally not larger than 200 ms, further optionally not larger than 20 ms, further optionally not larger than 1 ms. 13. The method of any one of claims 9 - 12, further comprising: - providing a closed-loop control (6099) of a temperature associated with the overheating event (221 ) based on said incrementally implementing of the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2). 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the uplink control signal (2002) is native to the Layer 1 or the Layer 2 of a communication protocol stack of the wireless link (1 1 1 ). 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the uplink control signal (2002) is piggybacked on a Layer 1 automatic repeat request control message, a Layer 2 Medium Access CE message, or a positive / negative acknowledgement message. 16. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one uplink control signal (2002) comprises a sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002), wherein the method further comprises: - communicating an uplink control message (2020) indicative of the overheating event (221 ), - triggering said communicating of the sequence (2099) of uplink control signals (2002) in response to said communicating of the uplink control message (2020). 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the uplink control message (2020) is native to a higher layer of a communication protocol stack of the wireless link (1 1 1 ) if compared to the native layer of the at least one uplink control signal (2002). 18. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first data (2001 ) is communicated according to a first modulation and/or coding and/or MIMO scheme, wherein the second data (2001 ) is communicated according to a second modulation and/or coding and/or MIMO scheme, wherein the first modulation and/or coding and/or MIMO scheme is the same as the second modulation and/or coding and/or MIMO scheme. 19. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the uplink control signal (2002) is piggybacked on an automatic repeat request control message. 20. A device (101 , 102) comprising control circuitry (101 1 , 1012, 1015, 1021 , 1022, 1025) configured to: - communicate, on a wireless link (1 1 1 ) between a network node (101 ) and a communication device (102), first data (2001 ) at a first data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2), 

 - communicate, on the wireless link (111), at least one uplink control signal (2002) associated with an overheating event (221) at the communication device (102), and - in response to said communicating of the at least one uplink control signal (2002): communicate, on the wireless link (111), second data (2001 ) at a second data rate (210,210-1,210-2), wherein the second data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2) is different from the first data rate (210, 210-1 , 210-2). 21. The device of claim 20, wherein the control circuitry is configured to perform the method of any one of claims 1 -19.
